title: Wire selectinload + extend routes for tags / search / filter
description:
# REPAIR NOTES

From CodeReviewer (hallucinated / unresolved symbols + missing error handling):
> `backend/app/api/routes/recipes.py:236` [function_call] `list_my_recipes_endpoint` — Spec mandates `?q=<query>` and `?tag=<tag_name>` (repeatable) query parameters with combined AND semantics. The handler signature accepts none of them, the `Query` import is dead, and `_escape_like_pattern` in repositories/recipes.py:63 is never called.

> `backend/app/api/routes/recipes.py:1` [function_call] `list_my_tags / GET /api/v1/recipes/tags` — No handler exists. The frontend `listMyTags()` will hit the `/{recipe_id}` parameterised handler with `recipe_id='tags'`, which `_validate_recipe_id` rejects as 400 invalid_recipe_id. `useMyTags` then sets tagLoadError=true and the TagFilter is hidden on every page load.

> `backend/app/api/routes/recipes.py:217` [function_call] `create_recipe / update_recipe tag upsert + link writes` — The router never reads `payload.tags`, never calls any tag repository helper. RecipeCreate's `tags` field is declared with `Field(exclude=True)` so `model_dump()` correctly omits it, but the result is that tags submitted from the form are silently dropped on the server.

> `backend/app/models/recipe.py:117` [attribute] `Recipe.tags relationship + selectinload contract` — The relationship is declared with `lazy='noload'`. None of the existing read paths use `selectinload(Recipe.tags)`, so RecipeOut.tags will always serialize as `[]` even after tag-link rows exist.

Reviewer recommendation:
> Change `Recipe.tags` to `lazy='select'` OR — better — keep `lazy='noload'` and add `.options(selectinload(Recipe.tags))` to every read path (`get_recipe_for_owner`, `list_recipes_for_owner`, the update helper's RETURNING fetch). The noload+selectinload combination is the only one that's both async-safe AND populates the response.

> Read `payload.tags` (Pydantic exposes it through `payload.tags` even though `Field(exclude=True)` hides it from `model_dump()`) in the create/update handlers. Upsert tag rows, link them via recipe_tags, ALL inside the same `db.run_sync` transaction so a failure rolls back the recipe row too. Emit the `recipe_tags_changed` audit event after the commit.

> Verify with an integration run that opening /my-recipes lands a successful GET /api/v1/recipes/tags (200 []) and that creating a recipe with tags=['Dinner'] makes the chip visible on detail. Today the frontend will: (a) hit 400 on /tags and hide the TagFilter, (b) successfully POST a recipe but never see chips. Fix the order: ORM relationship + selectinload first, then route extension, then tags writes.

The load-bearing security property called out by QualityEngineer:
> The single most important missing test is the cross-user PUT tag-side-effect leak: user B PUTs user A's recipe with tags=['NewTagOnlyB'] → 404 AND verify user B's /tags namespace does NOT include 'NewTagOnlyB' (the tag upsert MUST happen AFTER the ownership-gated UPDATE, not before).

## What to change

### 1) `backend/app/repositories/recipes.py`

(a) Extend `get_recipe_for_owner` to use `.options(selectinload(Recipe.tags))` on the existing owner-scoped SELECT. Keep the WHERE clause `id=:recipe_id AND owner_id=:owner_id` — the cross-user 404 invariant is preserved. When no row matches, return `None` WITHOUT triggering a tag SELECT (selectinload naturally short-circuits on empty parent result).

(b) Extend `list_recipes_for_owner` signature: `(session, *, owner_id, q: str | None = None, required_tag_ids: list[UUID] | None = None) -> list[Recipe]`.
  - Add the missing imports (`selectinload`, `func`, `exists`, `text`) at the top — these are documented in BE-006-U2 as still pending.
  - Always include `WHERE recipes.owner_id = :owner_id`.
  - Always include `.options(selectinload(Recipe.tags))` so every returned recipe carries tags.
  - When `q` (caller-trimmed, non-empty):
    * Build a parameterized pattern via `'%' + _escape_like_pattern(q) + '%'`, passed with `escape='\\'` (or however the helper documented in BE-006-U1 emits its escapes).
    * OR across three predicates: `Recipe.title.ilike(pattern, escape='\\')`, `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM jsonb_array_elements_text(Recipe.ingredients) AS ing WHERE ing ILIKE :pattern)` (use `text()` or a `func.jsonb_array_elements_text(...)`-based exists), `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM recipe_tags rt JOIN tags t ON t.id=rt.tag_id WHERE rt.recipe_id=Recipe.id AND (t.display_name ILIKE :pattern OR t.name ILIKE :pattern))`.
    * Apply `.distinct()` on the recipes selection to collapse multi-path matches.
  - When `required_tag_ids` non-empty: AND semantics — `WHERE recipes.id IN (SELECT recipe_id FROM recipe_tags WHERE tag_id = ANY(:ids) GROUP BY recipe_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT tag_id) = :n)`.
  - Ordering: `ORDER BY recipes.created_at DESC, recipes.id DESC`. Pagination still out of scope.
  - Defaults preserve milestone-2 behavior (regression check on the no-filter path).

(c) If milestone-2 also has an `update_recipe_for_owner` helper that re-fetches the row after UPDATE...RETURNING, make sure that re-fetch path also applies `selectinload(Recipe.tags)` so the PUT response carries the post-diff tag set. If the route handler does a separate `get_recipe_for_owner` after the update for the response, this is already covered by (a).

### 2) `backend/app/api/routes/recipes.py`

Declaration order: `GET /tags` MUST appear BEFORE any `/{recipe_id}` route. Add a one-line comment explaining why.

(a) Add `_normalize_tag_param(raw: str) -> str | None` private helper: strip leading/trailing whitespace; empty after strip → `None` (drop silently); embedded `\n`/`\r` or length > 50 → `raise HTTPException(400, 'invalid_query_param')` so the BE-006-fix1 global envelope handler formats it; otherwise return `raw.strip().lower()`.

(b) `GET /api/recipes/tags` — NEW handler. Auth via the same `get_current_user` + `require_roles(['user','admin'])` dependencies. Body: call `list_tags_with_counts_for_owner(session, owner_id=jwt.sub)`; map `(Tag, recipe_count)` to `TagSummary` (from `app.schemas.tag`); return `list[TagSummary]`. Always 200; empty user → `[]`. Ignore unknown query params (FastAPI default).

(c) `POST /api/recipes` — wire tag persistence.
  1. The validated `RecipeCreate` has already trimmed/deduped/validated `payload.tags` (BA-fix1-1 / BE-003-U4). Access via `payload.tags` even though `Field(exclude=True)` hides it from `model_dump()`.
  2. Inside the EXISTING transaction (or the same `db.run_sync` block):
     - INSERT the recipe (existing milestone-2 path).
     - If `payload.tags == []`: skip tag work entirely; reload + return.
     - Else: build `tag_inputs = [(t.lower(), t) for t in payload.tags]` (validator already deduped, so each lowercased name is unique post-validation).
     - `name_to_id = upsert_tags_for_owner(session, owner_id=jwt.sub, tag_inputs=tag_inputs)`.
     - `link_tags_to_recipe(session, recipe_id=created.id, tag_ids=name_to_id.values())`.
  3. Commit. On exception, the whole transaction rolls back (recipe + tag rows + links).
  4. Reload via `get_recipe_for_owner` so the response is `RecipeOut` with `.tags` populated (selectinload from step 1 above). Return 201.
  5. After commit, emit BOTH the milestone-2 `recipe_created` audit event AND a `recipe_tags_changed` event when `len(payload.tags) > 0` — body `{event, user_id, recipe_id, added_count: <N>, removed_count: 0, request_id, ts}` per the logging cross-cutting; do NOT log raw tag values.

(d) `PUT /api/recipes/{recipe_id}` — wire tag diff. CRITICAL ordering rule:
  1. Run the ownership-gated `update_recipe_for_owner(...)` first. If 0 rows affected → raise `HTTPException(404, 'recipe_not_found')` IMMEDIATELY. **Do NOT call `upsert_tags_for_owner` before this point** — otherwise user B's PUT against user A's recipe would create rows in B's tag namespace before the 404 returns. This is the load-bearing cross-user-tag-side-effect property called out by QE.
  2. After the ownership check passes, in the same transaction:
     - Treat omitted / `None` / `[]` for `payload.tags` identically as 'drop all links'.
     - Compute removed_count by counting current links before replacement (single SELECT or a `RETURNING count` from the DELETE — keep it simple).
     - Build `tag_inputs` and call `upsert_tags_for_owner` (no-op when empty).
     - Call `replace_tag_links_for_recipe(session, recipe_id=recipe_id, target_tag_ids=set(name_to_id.values()))`.
  3. Commit. Reload via `get_recipe_for_owner` for the response. Return 200.
  4. Emit `recipe_updated` (existing) + `recipe_tags_changed` (added_count, removed_count) when added+removed > 0.

(e) `GET /api/recipes/mine` — add `q: Optional[str] = Query(default=None, max_length=200)` and `tag: Optional[list[str]] = Query(default=None)` to the signature. FastAPI's 422 on q>200 routes through the BE-006-fix1 global handler → 400 envelope.
  1. Trim `q`; if empty/whitespace, treat as `None`.
  2. For each value in `tag` list (when provided), run `_normalize_tag_param` (drops blanks, raises 400 for oversize/newline).
  3. Resolve each normalized name to a tag id via `find_tag_id_by_name_for_owner`. If ANY lookup returns `None` → respond `200 []` immediately (spec: unknown tag → empty array, not 404, not 400).
  4. Call `list_recipes_for_owner(session, owner_id=jwt.sub, q=q_normalized, required_tag_ids=resolved_ids)`.
  5. Return 200 + `list[RecipeSummary]` — each row's `tags` field is populated via the eager-load.
  6. Unknown query params: ignored (FastAPI default).
  7. Add a one-line comment reaffirming: `# All queries scoped to owner_id; tag-name resolution is also owner-scoped — no cross-tenant leak.`

## Don't touch
- Milestone-2 success-path behavior for the unchanged branches (delete, the no-filter list case).
- The global RequestValidationError / HTTPException / OperationalError handlers (BE-006-fix1) — they already format the envelopes correctly.
- `delete_recipe` route — unchanged this milestone (recipe_tags cascade via FK).
- The `Recipe.tags` relationship's `lazy='noload'` setting per the reviewer recommendation — `selectinload` opt-in on every read path is the chosen pattern.
success_criteria: `get_recipe_for_owner` returns a Recipe with `.tags` populated (verified via attribute access not triggering a separate query) — selectinload short-circuits when the parent result is empty (cross-user None case does not issue a tags query).
`list_recipes_for_owner(owner_id, q=None, required_tag_ids=None)` is byte-identical to milestone-2 behavior with `.tags` eager-loaded on each row.
`list_recipes_for_owner(owner_id, q='salt')` matches recipes whose title, ingredient (via `jsonb_array_elements_text`), OR tag's name/display_name contains 'salt' (case-insensitive), DISTINCT.
`list_recipes_for_owner(owner_id, required_tag_ids=[id1,id2])` returns only recipes linked to BOTH ids (AND semantics).

POST `/api/recipes` with `tags: ['Dinner','dinner','DINNER']` returns 201 + `response.tags` of length 1 with `display_name='Dinner'`, `name='dinner'`. POST with `tags: []` returns 201 with `response.tags == []`. POST without a `tags` key returns 201 (milestone-2 regression check).
POST validation 400 matrix: > 20 unique tags, empty-after-trim, > 50 chars, embedded newline, non-string → each returns 400 with `tags` in `field_errors` envelope.
POST rollback: when `upsert_tags_for_owner` is patched to raise, the entire transaction rolls back — no recipe row and no orphan tag rows for the user.
PUT replaces tags fully: seed `['a','b']`, PUT `['b','c']` → response and subsequent GET both show `['b','c']`; tag 'a' still exists in `/tags`.

PUT cross-user 404 with tag-side-effect guard: user B's PUT against user A's recipe with `tags=['NewTagOnlyB']` returns 404 AND user B's GET `/api/recipes/tags` does NOT include 'NewTagOnlyB' (load-bearing — upsert must happen AFTER ownership-gated UPDATE).
GET `/api/recipes/{id}` for an owned recipe with tags returns `response.tags` populated; for a tagless recipe returns `response.tags == []`. Cross-user GET returns 404.

GET `/api/recipes/mine?q=...` filters via title/ingredient/tag using parameterized ILIKE; SQL meta-characters are safely escaped (`q='50%' off'` returns the matching recipe; injection payloads do not damage the table).

GET `/api/recipes/mine?tag=dinner&tag=easy` returns recipes carrying BOTH tags; `?tag=neverused` returns `200 []`; `?q=<201-char-string>` → 400; `?tag=<51-char-string>` or `?tag=<value-with-newline>` → 400; `?unknown=foo` is ignored.
Cross-user isolation: user B's GET `/api/recipes/mine?tag=dinner` returns `200 []` even when user A has a recipe tagged 'dinner' (B has no 'dinner' in their namespace).
Each row in the GET `/api/recipes/mine` response carries `tags: list[TagOut]` (populated or `[]`).

GET `/api/recipes/tags` for a fresh user returns 200 `[]`; for a user with two recipes sharing 'Dinner' and one with 'Easy' returns two TagSummary entries (`Dinner` recipe_count=2, `Easy` recipe_count=1) ordered case-insensitively by display_name; orphan tags (links removed but row remains) surface with `recipe_count=0`.
Route ordering regression: GET `/api/recipes/tags` resolves to the tags handler (asserted by response shape); GET `/api/recipes/not-a-uuid` still returns 400 `invalid_recipe_id`.

`recipe_tags_changed` audit event fires after a successful POST with non-empty tags and after a successful PUT where added+removed > 0; body contains `added_count`/`removed_count` and does NOT log raw tag values.
